summary: Regression backend suite after removal of emergentintegrations==0.2.0 and litellm (customer-assets URL) from backend/requirements.txt. All 12 targeted regression tests PASS. Auth (admin & super_admin), GET/POST /api/appuntamenti (with slot validation + Brevo email trigger returning HTTP 201), GET /api/reports/appuntamenti.pdf (valid PDF), GET /api/leads (super_admin) + 403 for admin, GET /api/studio/quota, POST /api/docenti quota enforcement, and APScheduler startup log all verified. No ModuleNotFoundError/ImportError for the removed libs in supervisor logs.

minor.issue: One historical 401 'unrecognised IP address' from Brevo detected at 15:48:28 (infra IP not whitelisted on Brevo panel); post-whitelisting the very next requests returned 201. Not related to the dependency removal — pre-existing/environmental.

action_items: None. Deployment-only fix confirmed non-regressive. Safe to redeploy on Railway.
OPTIONAL: verify Brevo authorised-IPs panel includes current backend egress IP (35.184.53.215) to avoid the intermittent 401 already observed pre-existing this iteration.
critical_code_review_comments: requirements.txt is clean: no emergentintegrations, no litellm URL entry.
grep across /app/backend/**/*.py confirms zero imports of emergentintegrations or litellm — safe removal.
email_service.py uses httpx directly against api.brevo.com (verified in logs).
reminder_scheduler.py uses APScheduler; startup log 'Reminder scheduler avviato' present after every supervisor restart.
server.py at 1845 lines is above the 700-line guideline — consider splitting into routers (auth, appuntamenti, studios, docenti, clienti, leads, reports) for maintainability (non-blocking).
test_credentials: admin@demo.it / Admin123! and superadmin@eligehub.it / SuperAdmin123! — both work; JWT returned as access_token.
seed_data_creation: One temp appuntamento and one temp docente were created and cleaned up (DELETE) at end of each test. Used 'TEST_iter17_regression' as note marker and 'test_iter17_<uuid>@demo.it' emails.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Regression suite lives at /app/backend/tests/test_iter17_deps_removal_regression.py — reuse for future deps changes. Run with: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=<url> pytest backend/tests/test_iter17_deps_removal_regression.py -v. Studio demo id = 95064d29-4598-4be5-abfc-4ee57bcbd4d8, plan=business, tipologia=centro_studi. /api/auth/me returns nested {user, studio}. /api/studio/quota uses 'professionisti_used'/'professionisti_limit' keys (not 'docenti_*'). /api/auth/login response key is 'access_token'.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bug found. Removal of the two unused dependencies from requirements.txt is safe: (1) no source files import them, (2) all critical endpoints still return expected status codes and payloads, (3) scheduler and Brevo integration both work with existing httpx+APScheduler stack. Railway deploy failure was purely a PyPI-availability problem (emergentintegrations==0.2.0 not on public PyPI; litellm was pinned to a customer-assets URL that isn't reachable outside Emergent infra). requirements.txt is now installable in any environment.
